prompt: Create a delicate ink wash and watercolor portrait of a young East Asian woman in right-facing side profile, eyes closed, serene and introspective. She has black hair swept dramatically backward to the left, merging into loose watery ink splashes, fine stray strands, and branching brush lines. Embed exactly one cluster of pale cherry blossom branches through the back of her hair, with small white and blush-pink flowers, buds, and muted olive-gray leaves. She wears a loose, translucent white kimono-like robe with soft gray ink shadows and minimal detail. Use an off-white textured paper background with visible watercolor grain. The composition is vertical, centered slightly to the right, leaving negative space around the head and ink bloom. Style: traditional Asian sumi-e / ink wash mixed with modern watercolor portraiture, monochrome grays with subtle peach skin tones and blush accents, splattered ink droplets, feathered wet-on-wet edges, fine pencil-like facial contour lines, elegant, airy, poetic. No text, no border, no watermark, no photorealistic background.
